scenario.stack: Microservices, Kong vs Custom Node.js Gateway
scenario.problem: Organization needed a highly scalable API Gateway to handle multi-tenant routing, rate limiting, and mTLS. Engineering wanted to build a custom solution from scratch in Go/Node to maintain 'total flexibility'. Business wanted it live in 6 weeks.

implementation.tradeoff_analysis: Ran a direct Build vs Buy evaluation.
implementation.build_option: Cost: $250k (developer time). Speed: 14 weeks. Quality: High context, but untested scale. Flexibility: Absolute.
implementation.buy_option: Kong Enterprise. Cost: $40k/year. Speed: 3 weeks. Quality: Battle-tested. Flexibility: High, but restricted by plugin ecosystem.

results.business_impact: Saved 11 weeks of time-to-market. The internal engineering team (12 engineers) was redirected to build the core fraud-detection engine, which was the actual competitive advantage.

lessons: Never build what you can buy if it doesn't give you a direct market advantage over competitors. API Gateways are commodity infrastructure.
Engineers inherently bias towards 'Build'. Technical Product Management must force the TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) conversation to save the business from reinventing the wheel.
anti_patterns: Not It Here (NIH) Syndrome: The belief that external tools are inherently inferior to internal code.
